sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the pattern clearly:
1. The purple square moved from ~(53,10) to ~(51,12) - it moved up the diagonal path by one G dot position!
2. The G dot at (51,12) was consumed (it's no longer in the G dots list)
3. A new G dot appeared at (53,10) where the purple square was before
4. The timer bar decreased by 2 more pixels (60 → 58)
So the mechanic is:
- Click on a G dot along the diagonal path to move the purple square to that position
- The G dot gets consumed and a new one appears where the purple square was
- The timer bar decreases with each action
The goal is likely to move the purple square all the way up the diagonal path to the blue shape at the top-right. Let me count the G dots remaining and plan the sequence.
Current G dots (from bottom to top along the path):
- (57,6), (55,8), (53,10), (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)
The purple square is now at (51,12). The next G dot up the path would be (49,14).
Wait, let me reconsider. The purple square is at ~(51,12) which is between (53,10) and (49,14) on the diagonal. The pattern of G dots is:
- Each step goes up 2 rows and right 2 cols
- (57,6) → (55,8) → (53,10) → (51,12) → (49,14) → ...
So the next G dot to click would be (49,14), then (47,16), etc., all the way to the blue shape.
Let me count: from (49,14) to (17,46), that's:
(49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)
That's 17 more steps to reach the blue shape area. But the timer bar only has 58 pixels left, losing 2 per action, so 29 more actions possible. That should be enough.
But wait - I need to think about what happens when the purple square reaches the blue shape. The blue shape has a G center at ~(15,48). The last G dot is at (17,46), so after clicking that, the purple square would be at (17,46), which is adjacent to the blue shape.
Actually, let me reconsider the goal. Maybe the goal is to move the purple square to the blue shape's position, or maybe there's something else. Let me just continue moving the purple square up the path and see what happens.
I should batch the clicks to be efficient. Let me click on all the remaining G dots in sequence.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The purple square moved from (53,10) to (51,12) - up the diagonal path!
# The G dot at (51,12) was consumed, and a new G dot appeared at (53,10).
# The mechanic: click a G dot to move the purple square there.
# Goal: move the purple square all the way to the blue shape.

# Current purple square position: ~(51,12)
# G dots remaining along the path (going up toward blue shape):
# (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)

# Let me batch click all remaining G dots to move the purple square to the blue shape
g_dots_path = [
    (49, 14), (47, 16), (45, 18), (43, 20), (41, 22), (39, 24), (37, 26),
    (35, 28), (33, 30), (31, 32), (29, 34), (27, 36), (25, 38), (23, 40),
    (21, 42), (19, 44), (17, 46)
]

actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in g_dots_path]
print(f"Executing {len(actions)} clicks along the diagonal path...")
action(actions)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
